Invest in WHAT? Our oil? Even the companies who already own all the oil extraction in this country won't invest more. The government had to step in and BUY the last wildly unpopular pipeline, and is STILL running it at a huge loss. Our mining sector is a top-to-bottom scam. Invest in WHAT?

We don't have the labour force to meet existing construction demands because construction companies refuse to pay a living wage to workers. Retail is *dead*. Heavy industry is stagnant at best. Logging has been practised so unsustainably for so long the whole sector is pentafucked forever.

What else can we invest in? Agriculture? Good luck! Only dairy makes any money and you can't get in to the dairy sector because the supply management that makes it profitable also makes it impossible to break in to that sector. Nothing else makes money because export markets are too unstable.

Telecom is a protectionist racket, so there's no foreign investment there. Electricity generation is a possible entrant, but the hurdles there are regulatory (fuck you, Alberta), so no sane investor wants to lay down hard cash to invest there.

We're 40M people, so investing in services is stupid, because we can't sell services to the enemy nation to the south, nor is there a point in selling them to any of our actual allies because they're either hyperprotectionist (EU) or just ridiculously outnumber us (CPTPP).

What should foreign investors invest IN? They have trillions of dollars, sure, but investors demand a return on that investment. Canadians don't have any fucking money so investing in something to cater to 40 Million broke-ass peasants is a dumb idea.

Despite this, there is no room left to drive down wages in Canada, so we're basically worthless from an export standpoint because everything we could possibly do for exports that isn't raw materials can be done more cheaply elsewhere, and we can't export raw materials because of rampant corruption.

Sucking foreign investor dick isn't going to magically solve Canada's problems. I know Carney doesn't want to pay for the infrastructure that Canada desperately needs to upgrade, but private companies are NOT going to pay for Canadians to get infrastructure without ROI we simply CANNOT guarantee.

Do you really think some highway into the middle of nowhere in NWT to build a port is going to happen without it being 100% government funded? You're insane. Sit the fuck back down.  How can the we afford it? Tax the rich. Clamp down on tax avoidance and corruption. Regulate captured industries.

We don't NEED foreign investment to do things. What we need to do is hunt down the parasites sucking all of the capital out of our economy and lock them up FOREVER. We need to keep doing that until ordinary peasant Canadians have money to spend on shit again. ONLY THAT will get the economy going.

Trickle-down is a failure, and Carney needs to wake up to that. His austerity addiction is what is killing the economy. His oligarch buddies are the bloodsuckers killing us all.  Our path to economic independence starts by locking up the parasites, not prostrating ourselves before even more of them.

i thought a lot about music. how if/when i make music, i won't stop being a poet and visual artist. so naturally i will be using my existing talents to make music, i don't have to learn an entirely new skill with no foundations even if that is in essence what i am doing.
i mean that my music will, at first at least, feature my poetry, me reading my poetry. that i need to make music that is me reading my poetry with a soundscape or a sick beat,

and that it will probably be weird and esoteric and not very listenable for the average person who doesn't care about poetry. might be a lot more about self expression than sounding good and that that's okay.
i need to welcome experimentation and play into this discipline because that is how i learn. and i won't learn unless i make some things that sound just... weird and abstract.
and maybe i will like that a lot actually. i might not become a "proper" producer who makes "edm" but i will become a musician.
